art_market.summary: Art Basel Hong Kong 2026 (Mar 27-29) continues. Resilience noted amidst economic backdrop; sales include Picasso (€3.5M), Liu Ye ($3.8M), and Dumas ($3.5M). Government commits to 5-year hosting agreement.
top_stories: Steady sales reported despite global market volatility; notable works by Picasso, Liu Ye, and Marlene Dumas sold.
Hong Kong government enters 5-year hosting agreement with Art Basel to strengthen local art ecosystem.
Continued emphasis on Asian-Pacific artists and digital art initiatives like 'Zero 10'.

stock_market.summary: KOSPI plunged 3.03% to 5,256.46 as Middle East tensions and concerns over Google's 'TurboQuant' AI technology heavily impact memory semiconductor manufacturers.
key_factors: Renewed Middle East tensions between US/Israel and Iran drive oil price surge (Brent +5.66%) and market instability.
Google's 'TurboQuant' algorithm threatens semiconductor demand; Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix lead declines.
Risk-off sentiment prevails despite diplomatic attempts for ceasefire.
